title: F-004 install + F-006 error 换皮 (ConsoleOutputRenderer 用结构基元) + NO_COLOR snapshot
description: Reskin the install renderer packages/cli/src/tui/ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts to the W3-B structural language: renderStep/renderSection use section-bar + tree (├─/└─ stages with [ok] badges per mockup #2); renderSummaryCard uses section-bar (▌ Summary) + grid counts; renderError (F-006, same file) becomes a badge title (▌ [err] <Title>) + a left-bar (│) grouped block per mockup #4. The OutputRenderer interface (tui/types.ts) is unchanged — only the rendering engine. Extract pure builder fns and ship NO_COLOR snapshots covering install steps+summary AND error. Bundles F-004 and F-006 because both live in this one renderer.
action: In ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts: (1) replace boldAccent() header with sectionBar(); (2) accumulate steps and render them as a tree() with per-step status badge + (current/total) per mockup #2 (or render each step as a tree branch line); (3) renderSummaryCard → sectionBar("Summary") + grid of ✓ succeeded / ○ skipped / × failed counts; (4) renderError → first line `sectionBar('[err] '+title)` (badge in title), then a left-bar block: each of message / hint / stack-frame prefixed with painted '│ ' (ASCII '| '), per mockup #4. Import sectionBar from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/theme, tree/grid from ./structure.js, keep existing paint/symbol/isColorEnabled imports. Extract pure builders (buildStepLine/buildSummaryBlock/buildErrorBlock) usable by the snapshot test. Do NOT change tui/types.ts interface signatures.

criteria: ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts imports sectionBar from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/theme and tree/grid from ./structure.js (grep both)
renderError renders a badge title + left-bar block: grep -E 'sectionBar\(|\\u2502|\"\\u2502 \"|tree\(|grid\(' shows structural primitives in ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts (>=1 hit)
the OutputRenderer interface in tui/types.ts is unchanged (git diff packages/cli/src/tui/types.ts is empty)
a NO_COLOR snapshot test covering install steps+summary AND error block exists and passes (toMatchSnapshot, NO_COLOR=1)
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- ConsoleOutputRenderer && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit
convergence.definition_of_done: install pipeline output and error output render via section-bar + tree + grid + left-bar group per mockups #2 and #4; the OutputRenderer interface is unchanged; both surfaces are snapshot-pinned for NO_COLOR.

files.change: Swap flat composition for sectionBar + tree (stages) + grid (summary counts) + left-bar error block; extract pure build* helpers; preserve OutputRenderer interface.
Snapshot buildStepLine sequence + buildSummaryBlock + buildErrorBlock over fixtures under NO_COLOR=1.

implementation: Map mockup #2 (install tree + summary grid) and mockup #4 (error badge title + left-bar block) to renderStep/renderSummaryCard/renderError.
Extract pure builders: buildStepLine(step), buildSummaryBlock(summary), buildErrorBlock(info) returning strings.
Rewrite the render* methods to compose via sectionBar + tree + grid + painted '│ ' left-bar, gating ASCII fallback through structure.ts/isColorEnabled.
Add install-renderer-reskin.test.ts with NO_COLOR snapshots for both install and error.

reference.pattern: W3-A ConsoleOutputRenderer pure string composition; establish a NEW vitest toMatchSnapshot test with NO_COLOR=1 (grouped-help.test.ts uses toContain/toMatch, not toMatchSnapshot, and does not pin NO_COLOR — do not reuse it; add a fresh NO_COLOR=1 snapshot test)

rationale.chosen_approach: F-004 (install) and F-006 (error) are bundled because renderError lives inside ConsoleOutputRenderer — same file, same renderer, same import set. One feature-grouped task avoids a redundant agent spawn over the same module.
decision_factors: one feature = one task; both surfaces in one file
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly larger task, but cohesive — both surfaces share the same primitive wiring.

risks: renderStep is called per-step (streaming) while the mockup shows a final tree — accept a per-line tree-branch render (├─/└─ inferred from current==total) rather than buffering, to keep the streaming contract.
Touching tui/types.ts would break the install/pipeline seam — guarded by a git-diff criterion.
